139438384.title: SLT Huddle
139438384.body:
:clipboard: *SLT Huddle* — 2026-04-20 17:00 · 30m
_With:_ Gary Tuerack, Ashleigh Smith, Heather Darnell, Michael O'Brien, Kevin Prentiss, Adam Stone
https://fathom.video/share/dt5rB-nBDNq6SCtNo54D_Hsudv7yCLx-

*Takeaways*
• **Retreat Logistics Finalized:** The retreat agenda is set, including a working lunch on Wednesday and a tentative axe-throwing dinner at Gary's land. Anish will use Kevin's Zoom for hybrid sessions.
• **CS Team Goal Misalignment:** The CS team's spring goal is too high relative to its budget, creating a perception of underperformance. Anish will work with Ashley to reallocate goals for a more balanced fall.
• **AI Tools Show High ROI:** New AI tools are delivering immediate value. Josh's quote-puller automates content creation, and the SLT EA bot enabled Chelsea to ramp up in just two hours.

*Action items*
• Update Thu lunch order for Katie
• Send section invites via Kevin's Zoom; include Heather
• Invite Jordan to Thu meeting
• Email Heather re: contractor all-staff/monthly policy
• Email Anish + Heather re: Red's comp
• Send Summit Budget pre-read to SLT

139414780.title: Anish / Jordan / Adam
139414780.body:
:clipboard: *Anish / Jordan / Adam* — 2026-04-20 16:00 · 30m
_With:_ Adam Stone, jtannenbaum@nsls.org, Anish Patel
https://fathom.video/share/e6Aaux4DezPngum6sEAz9WxhvsgsiGry

*Takeaways*
• **SLT Presentation Confirmed:** Jordan will present the Q1/Q2 deck to SLT on Thursday at 11:15 AM ET, covering platform readiness, data infrastructure, and reporting capabilities.
• **Strategic Shift:** The core message is a shift from a reactive "data as a service" model to a proactive "AI enablement" model, where users self-serve with Hex AI and the data team acts as governors.
• **Chapter Health Score is Key:** This Q2 priority is the primary ROI driver. The goal is to save 10 at-risk chapters, a metric that repays the entire data investment.

*Action items*
• Email Danielle re: reschedule May 13 All Hands; if not, record demo + email
• Email Ashley re: dashboard hours saved + at-risk chapters goal; update deck
• Create SLT invite Thu 11:15 ET; add Jordan
• Update SLT deck: platform readiness + data infra; remove costs; add org chart; send pre-read to SLT
• Check w/ Lillian on data systems engineer offer status
• Add SLT dashboard to Q2 priorities slide

*Takeaways*
• **Devin's Plan Finalized:** Incentive pay is now set for March and September annually, with the first payment in September to accelerate his schedule.
• **Contractor Comms Overhaul:** To prevent sensitive info leaks (e.g., SARS), the team will propose a new Slack structure: `#all-staff` for W2s only and a separate, inclusive channel for general announcements.
• **New VP of Ops:** Chelsea Byers joins as fractional VP of Ops for a 90-day trial, with a potential conversion to a full-time W2 role.

*Action items*

138674026.title: SLT Huddle
138674026.body:
:clipboard: *SLT Huddle* — 2026-04-16 17:00 · 30m
_With:_ Gary Tuerack, Ashleigh Smith, Heather Darnell, Michael O'Brien, Kevin Prentiss, Adam Stone
https://fathom.video/share/d1yM5vtsXsVoYyyCEdiTEKskWCpCzrBn

*Takeaways*
• **Impact Day Budget Overrun:** The event's success (11k participants) caused a \~$60-70k budget overrun, driven by an uncapped spend on shipping. This highlighted a critical process failure and a need to re-evaluate the vendor relationship with Underground.
• **Product Roadmap Blocked:** Development of the "Freshness First" feature is blocked by the urgent OA System of Record project. While still on track for fall, this delay prevents a planned NCO response rate boost.
• **Strategic Partnership with Nexus:** A potential partnership with Nexus (a high-powered network) could provide a low-cost path to launch chapters at elite universities like Harvard and Columbia.

*Action items*
• Email teams: freshness visibility likely ready for fall
• Email Kimberly re: OA scope; align on OA vs freshness/invite automation
• Email Kimberly re: OA scope; align on OA vs freshness/invite automation
• Email SLT re: AI meeting assessment ratings
• Email Kevin re: David Adams announcement plan
• Email Kevin re: Signal/Quick Notes rollout (PD, RevOps, CS); exclude MEX

138637654.body:

_With:_ Heather Darnell, Gary Tuerack, Ashleigh Smith, Anish Patel
https://fathom.video/share/_KEg-Ee7dfiHRPtDFrsf6h4s86LLWp8E

*Takeaways*
• **Marissa Carey:** Approved for $170k base + 30% commission, resolving a pay mix miscommunication. This decision prioritizes retaining a key contributor over strict adherence to the internal pay structure.
• **Kimberly:** Approved for a $20k raise (to $115k) to recognize increased responsibilities. A future raise is contingent on improving soft skills (e.g., managing conflict with Royce) and taking on new projects.
• **Mel:** Identified as significantly underpaid ($170k vs. $209k 25th percentile benchmark) due to operating at a VP level with a Senior Director title. A "true-up" raise is now a priority.

*Action items*
• Confirm Roomzy corporate incentive plan level w/ Jenna
• Research Top Workplaces nomination; assess accreditation/business model
• Email Gary LexisNexis website link
• Email Ashleigh Kimberly bonus %; then Ashleigh/Heather align positioning/timing
• Text Marissa re: comp; offer call; then Heather send Rippling addendum (170+30)

137881659.title: SLT Standing Meeting
137881659.body:
:clipboard: *SLT Standing Meeting* — 2026-04-14 16:00 · 240m
_With:_ Michael O'Brien, Gary Tuerack, Ashleigh Smith, Kevin Prentiss, Adam Stone, Anish Patel
https://fathom.video/share/oi4UfBojEQR4ADzazsh35piicnMdZzVf

*Takeaways*
• **Member Experience Crisis:** Member Experience (MEX) is facing a crisis, with phone abandonment rates at 18.53% (vs. a 7% goal) and a ticket backlog of nearly 2,200. This is driven by high volume and inefficient processes, causing member frustration and refunds.
• **Strategic Alignment Framework:** The team approved the "Signal" framework to measure strategic alignment. It uses five metrics (Scorecard Coverage, Scorecard Health, Accountability, Comp Alignment, Quality of Hire) to create the data infrastructure needed for future AI-powered HR tools.
• **Q1 Financials & Q2 Goals:** Q1 core revenue hit $12.6M (vs. a $10M budget), driven by higher-than-budgeted invitations and response rates. This puts the company \~$300k ahead of spring revenue projections and on track to beat the annual EBITDA goal by a similar amount.

*Action items*
• Schedule Carta cap table walkthrough w/ Gary
• Fix Carta cap table valuation calc (115M vs 100M)
• Email SLT re: S&T mentorship proposal
• Remove 'skip steps' from program slides
• Grab Ashleigh's productivity testimonial video
• Send MEX deck link to SLT

137844722.title: Manager Preview Meeting
137844722.body:
:clipboard: *Manager Preview Meeting* — 2026-04-14 15:00 · 60m
_With:_ Ashleigh Smith, Adam Stone, Danielle Mariani, Gary Tuerack, Heather Darnell, Hannah Stoneburner
https://fathom.video/share/Lkp-4xwTD6A8Vy8NMc16NuP8PHJLEgNK

*Takeaways*
• **SARs for All:** Stock Appreciation Rights (SARs) are now for all full-time employees, not just a select group. This retroactive grant (Jan 1, 2026) aims to align everyone with long-term company growth, especially from the new subscription platform, Society.
• **Society Platform Launch:** The "Society" platform is launching to current members post-induction, using AI to help them define career paths. A free version will expand the addressable market from the top 30% of students to the entire school population.
• **Chapter Leader Roadmap:** A new roadmap will proactively communicate upcoming changes to chapter leaders, ensuring they are informed and engaged with NSLS's strategic direction.

*Action items*
• Post tomorrow's breakout question in Slack today
• Schedule SARs office hours w/ Anish; send SARs explainer deck to staff
• Schedule SARs office hours w/ Anish; send SARs explainer deck to staff
• Send Chapter Leader Roadmap draft link in manager Slack; iterate slides based on feedback
• Update tomorrow's deck w/ real national events stats + member/chapter stories
• Create Society Slack channel; share weekly sprint updates

137507865.title: SLT Huddle

https://fathom.video/share/6JVTTL96WD2nei2u_DznwGU3u-CxbgMy

*Takeaways*
• **Spring Revenue Forecast:** We are on track to beat our spring revenue goal by $300k–$800k, a range dependent on new school/alumni top-of-funnel additions.
• **Critical Mobile Web Fixes:** Performance issues and a persistent Apple Pay bug on Drupal were identified. This makes the off-season migration of all reactive flows from Drupal to React an even higher priority.
• **Banking Relationship at Risk:** Our entire CMB banking team is leaving, jeopardizing our line of credit. We must now decide whether to follow them to a new, smaller bank or find a new partner.

*Action items*
• Sync w/ Kevin re: auto-grader feature request
• Update Airtable w/ pre-vacation work
• Discuss CMB banking relationship/LOC w/ Gary
• Consult William re: banking relationship options
• Confirm retreat Fri venue w/ Katie; update team
• Follow up w/ Michael to set up personal productivity system

_With:_ Adam Stone, jtannenbaum@nsls.org, Anish Patel
https://fathom.video/share/kfccxNtrcH1EN4TyaGnYgWhSySENzats

*Takeaways*
• **Data Foundation Approved:** A 4-week contractor engagement is approved to build a reusable data foundation (tables, AI skills), enabling consistent metrics across all dashboards and deprecating the costly Redshift instance.
• **Snowflake Contract Strategy:** Jordan will negotiate a multi-year Snowflake contract, bundling Hex to leverage the Marketplace Capacity Drawdown Program and secure higher discounts. The goal is to sign before Snowflake's quarter-end.
• **Revenue Forecast Corrected:** The revenue forecast will be adjusted down \~$300k by applying a 90% "haircut" to "expected remaining" revenue, aligning the dashboard with CS feedback on at-risk deals.

*Action items*
• Meet contractors Apr 13; refine SOW; route via HR
• Email Equals re: updated 3-mo invoice
• Email Snowflake multi-year pricing options; schedule review w/ Anish + Adam
• Analyze AWS cost spike; report findings
• Update SLT forecast: apply 90% haircut to 'expected remaining' + 'new chapters'
• Fix Hex dashboard: show Northbridge Alumni in 'not yet mailed'

https://fathom.video/share/WcH3T26ysgPRGKFBss3szQfG1YseLfkU

*Takeaways*
• **Sales Velocity Doubled:** Days to close non-resale deals halved from \~240 to 120, a major win for higher ed sales.
• **Comp Tool Decision Looms:** The Comp Analyst contract expires in early June. We must decide whether to renew or switch to a tech-focused alternative like Carta's tool.
• **Tech Blockers Emerge:** Snowflake refuses to contract with Florida-based companies, and Carta's Rippling integration is unavailable for LLCs, creating significant operational hurdles.

*Action items*

https://fathom.video/share/GBKnosXUdLgPyw8am9c3smXU9iuZu5ze

*Takeaways*
• **Quick Notes Expansion:** The Quick Notes bot, now technically stable (v11/v12), will roll out to Ashleigh's and Anish's teams. Kevin will introduce it as a tool for getting credit for work, not for monitoring.
• **LOP Reset:** The SLT will reset LOPs next week. Key changes include revising the "Total Enrollment" L2 to focus on "share of market" and moving the "Talent Acquisition" L2 to Q3 with new, specific metrics.
• **SLT Dashboard Priority:** The SLT Dashboard is the top priority for the data team, which is resource-constrained (Jordan is the sole analyst). This creates a direct trade-off with revenue-enabling data work.

*Action items*

• Move Launch Stakeholders Program L2 and response-rate L2 to Product Engagement

https://fathom.video/share/2r_SRru9A6LnHA-W3ugVNAXV-engFCGb

*Takeaways*
• **Staffing Crisis:** The data team is at critical capacity, with only Jordan available for ad-hoc requests. Calvin (CS) and Angelica (backfill) are dedicated to other teams, and Brandon is on an engineering project.
• **Agency Pivot:** The planned analyst hire is paused. An agency will be engaged for a 4-week AI Hex Studio engagement to build foundational data pipelines, enabling future self-service.
• **Strategic Reprioritization:** Focus is shifting from a 1:1 Feather dashboard migration to building three core dashboards (Founder, Chapter Health, Revenue) that answer key business questions.

*Action items*

135598828.body:

https://fathom.video/share/tqpErG3sgzxc3SG8b1JKSEc31pMjD3RT

*Takeaways*
• **Support Backlog:** A support backlog is driven by a 500% YoY increase in S\&T submission *impressions* (not completions) and a faulty auto-grader that requires manual review of all subsequent S\&Ts after a single failure.
• **Season Projections:** The $14.8M revenue goal is achievable but tight, as the peak enrollment wave has passed. The risk is a lagging support volume spike from the 12,500+ new members who joined during the extended deadline.
• **Strategic Initiatives:** Key projects include a $20k Niche test for high school contacts, a Q2 LOP reset to improve goal clarity, and deprecating all Drupal enrollment flows for a fully React-based system next season.

*Action items*

*Takeaways*
• **SARS Plan Finalizing:** The SARS plan is nearing completion with Carta as administrator; finalization awaits Gary and Corey's review of the broad allocation list.
• **High-Impact Tests Validated:** A mailer legitimacy test (adding Newsweek/NCCRS badges) yielded a +0.34% lift, worth over $1M annually. A\&E upsell button and GradSet bundle tests also showed significant, platform-specific ARPM gains.
• **AI Recruiting Blocker:** AI-generated resumes and interview manipulation are severely degrading the talent acquisition pipeline, making it nearly impossible to vet candidates.

*Action items*

132387965.title: SLT Standing Meeting (90-minutes)
132387965.body:

https://fathom.video/share/-SqUqUmDPdRGxEUVv5mx9VzMTyrBx_Qx

*Takeaways*
• **Automated Support Overhaul:** High member frustration with automated support (chatbot, KB) will be addressed by assigning Isaiah to lead a Q2 L3 project. The strategy is to escalate users to an AI agent after one failed attempt, not to rebuild the current chatbot.
• **New Shop Vendor Search:** The SLT will replace fulfillment partner Underground due to poor performance (no inventory counts, blame-shifting). Michael O'Brien will lead the search for a new vendor, with CampusDoc identified as a strong candidate for its co-branding licensing services.
• **Data Dictionary & Regalia Policy:** A new, standard data dictionary will be created to resolve confusion around key metrics (LTV, ARPM, AOV). Separately, the SLT will investigate the revenue impact of enforcing an induction-only regalia policy to protect the society's honor.

*Action items*
• Reschedule 30-min overlapping mtg w/ Amy
• Send Chelsea resume/LinkedIn to Ashleigh + Anish
• Flag Tatiana gift w/ Heather pre-Thu; finalize approach
• Collect Disney College Program context from Hannah + Stephanie; email Gary by Mar 30
• Update L2 enrollment goal language re: chapter health + retention
• Convene LTV/ARPM/AOV dictionary working group; propose definitions to SLT

130603143.title: SLT Standing Meeting
130603143.body:
:clipboard: *SLT Standing Meeting* — 2026-03-17 17:00 · 90m
_With:_ Gary Tuerack, Ashleigh Smith, Michael O'Brien, Kevin Prentiss, Adam Stone, Anish Patel
https://fathom.video/share/E1TS1SY7idsWRW4ir37U5tV7YiPy1zMx

*Takeaways*
• **New L2 for Chapter Health:** The "Grow total enrollment" L2 will be replaced with a "Chapter Health" L2. This consolidates disparate churn-prevention efforts into a single, measurable strategy, using a new health score to drive retention.
• **Split LTV Goal:** The combined $145 LTV L2 will be split into two separate L2s: one for enrollment ARPM and one for post-enrollment upsell revenue. This creates winnable goals by isolating performance from external factors.
• **Greenlit Initiatives:** The team approved two key initiatives: 1) reallocating an internal developer to run revenue-generating A/B tests in the member area, and 2) continuing the personal email campaign to capture significant revenue despite its operational complexity.

*Action items*
• Draft analysis sharing process; require SFDs routed via Jordan; add SFD/source headers
• Email Hannah re: NCO analysis; reset sharing norms
• Email Erin Adam Munz SOW
• Update SLT retreat invites: mark Mar 21 dinner optional; extend Mar 24 end time
• Draft Chapter Health L2; post wording options in Slack
• Send Fathom video to Ashley re: Chapter Health scoring edits

https://fathom.video/share/AYhP-ddASUvywaVnhS9KS_WBYcNEYE1V

*Takeaways*
• **iHeartMedia SEM ads launch next Monday,** targeting large schools outside major cities to maximize ROI. Michael will build end-to-end tracking to measure true value (application submissions, not just clicks).
• **The OA team transition is underway,** with internal comms rolling out today. Ashleigh will send a Client Services email announcement and lead a deep-dive on Wednesday.
• **The SPD team's new video-first hiring process is a success,** saving time and yielding higher-quality, more engaged intern candidates.

*Action items*
• Send Kevin list of Christine Pierce subscriptions; transfer business items before locking card
• Email Gary iHeart school list for approval; send to iHeart after
• Build end-to-end tracking for iHeart SEM w/ Andrew & Royce; include Typeform submission
• Email Ashleigh NASPA post-comms plan/next steps
• Email Heather & Danielle OA announcement draft for approval
• Email Katie SLT offsite booking details: hotel + separate venue (Foundry/gallery), same locations, board meeting, Nathan food

129561189.title: Jordan / Ashleigh
129561189.body:
:clipboard: *Jordan / Ashleigh* — 2026-03-12 20:00 · 60m
_With:_ Ashleigh Smith, Adam Stone, jtannenbaum@nsls.org, Anish Patel
https://fathom.video/share/mWxEvtN7BFDu6TS3vtGRwWc9mb6QjCsz

*Takeaways*
• **HubSpot as Source of Truth:** Adopted a long-term strategy to originate all chapter and checklist data in HubSpot, replacing the current Feather/Falcon system. This will eliminate unreliable data syncs and create a single source of truth.
• **Improved Forecasting:** Jordan will pull HubSpot deal data from Snowflake to forecast revenue for unqueued chapters. This will use the `Forecast Probability` field (e.g., \>90% for March) and `Deal Vertical` to map deals to the correct dashboard cohorts.
• **Critical Risk Identified:** The Q3 2026 Drupal migration is at risk. Without a clear plan and stakeholder input, Tech could rebuild legacy systems in a new environment, perpetuating the current data silos and operational friction.

*Action items*
• Start thread w/ Royce, David, Juan re: HubSpot-first; include Rachel after comms w/ Michael
• Add agenda item for Tue sync: Kevin in loop + Juan/David/Royce spike
• Email Calvin re: system architecture diagrams; then fill gaps w/ Juan/David
• Send Jordan HubSpot pipeline/stage/prob filters + resale list; then Jordan builds fuzzy-match forecast
• Run manual actual+anticipated update for SLT
• Coordinate w/ Marissa to send Cal State Dominguez alumni donation to foundation

128709232.title: SLT Standing Meeting
128709232.body:
:clipboard: *SLT Standing Meeting* — 2026-03-10 17:00 · 90m
_With:_ Gary Tuerack, Ashleigh Smith, Michael O'Brien, Kevin Prentiss, Adam Stone, Anish Patel
https://fathom.video/share/Ns5ftziDSSPyJy3FL1iHzKQuzSQ9gxYx

*Takeaways*
• **Standardized Response Rate:** Defined a new default for the headline response rate KPI to ensure consistent analysis, excluding NCO/Alumni chapters and segmenting by New vs. Continuing schools.
• **Contextualized Churn Data:** The recent churn report reflects a necessary cleanup of stale accounts, not a sudden performance drop. Ashleigh Smith will provide context to prevent misinterpretation before tactical action.
• **New Evergreen Agreements:** New evergreen chapter agreements will be rolled out by mid-April, with a 5-year contingency plan for schools unable to sign the primary evergreen terms.

*Action items*
• Start NCO disentanglement spike w/ David Adams + Brandon; deliver roadmap by Mar 31
• Publish response-rate defaults in Slack; update dashboard defaults w/ Jordan; add resale toggle
• Comment on churn report; meet w/ Jordan to refine next week
• Analyze national events impact on churn; surface actionable metrics
• Draft Evergreen project plan; form B2B sub-squad
• Send healthy-chapter requirements to attorney; include first-year T&Cs; request draft next week

128411000.title: Anish / Jordan / Adam
128411000.body:
:clipboard: *Anish / Jordan / Adam* — 2026-03-09 20:30 · 30m
_With:_ Adam Stone, jtannenbaum@nsls.org, Anish Patel
https://fathom.video/share/4vB7N7X1fP_2iUF1rcJb5Q9jKAPLeFzY

*Takeaways*
• **Problem:** The forecast is inflated by a single outlier, Atlanta University Center.
• **Cause:** A 10x increase in database size (94 → 3,700) is causing the model to extrapolate an unsustainably high response rate.
• **Impact:** This single chapter accounts for \~$1M of the $1.8M forecast from returning chapters.

*Action items*
• Send Anish chapter-level list (forecast + stage); then Anish cap post-extension
• Add subtotals to SLT summary sections
• Fix OTT exclude bug; restore match-day filter
• Build YoY response-rate report by category for board
• Confirm w/ Rachel/Voice: for-profit/non-profit + stamp tracking; then Jordan spike data sources
• Confirm w/ Rachel/Voice: for-profit/non-profit + stamp tracking; then Jordan spike data sources

https://fathom.video/share/RP7iztAZgEYUf16rmuzehEiaBA-yX-GT

*Takeaways*
• **Vendor Change:** Underground will be replaced after repeated failures (e.g., insufficient books, missing banners at NASPA), forcing a search for a new partner.
• **OA Team Transition Paused:** The transition is on hold pending a unit economics analysis to ensure the two new Mechs hires can cover the OA team's hours (est. 75% of total) and prevent business disruption.
• **Parent Engagement Strategy:** A new strategy to engage parents via university portals was launched at NASPA, with a pilot at Duquesne University, to boost response rates.

*Action items*

128221327.body:

_With:_ Jenna Fontanez, Heather Darnell, Valerie English, Erin Muskat, Anish Patel
https://fathom.video/share/_Q6jTtBdvNRFy5QhyUA7zzY-ZsLbsooy

*Takeaways*
• **Engineer Comp Discrepancy:** A $125k budget for a senior-level engineer JD is causing recruiting delays. The team will meet with Adam and Jordan to align on a higher, more competitive range.
• **Hiring Process Breakdown:** The hiring process is fragmented, with tech leadership using direct networks and contractors, bypassing HR. This undermines HR's LOP goal and creates role confusion.
• **New Comp Strategy:** HR will propose a new comp strategy to the SLT, replacing the current tool with a Rippling-integrated AI vendor and purchasing a dedicated tech salary survey for better data.

*Action items*

• Rerun comp analysis for engineer role; update JD if needed
• Schedule Heather to present comp vendor/survey options to SLT

https://fathom.video/share/aHipCgYADAqQW2RJMEa-qHSgTXrwaLqA

*Takeaways*
• **AI Adoption Strategy:** The meeting will use a positive framing ("favorite AI use") and a public "build-in-public" project in the `#ai-workbench` channel to encourage AI adoption.
• **New Walking Pad Benefit:** A new perk will be announced, offering reimbursement for compact walking pads (not full treadmills) to promote movement and focus. The benefit is retroactive for all of 2025.
• **Core Value Award Process Change:** The SLT will now have a vote in the Core Value Awards. This change ensures winners reflect strategic impact known to leadership, not just peer nominations.

*Action items*

• Disable newsletter auto-archive to see all sends

127538641.title: GT/AP/JT
127538641.body:
:clipboard: *GT/AP/JT* — 2026-03-05 17:00 · 60m
_With:_ Gary Tuerack, Adam Stone, jtannenbaum@nsls.org, Anish Patel
https://fathom.video/share/Gscz3PnYvxZdxY7e-eKgxVdm4zUaEudj

*Takeaways*
• **Data Discrepancy Resolved:** The Feather vs. BEHEX response rate gap is due to Feather excluding NCOs and using a transactional date, while BEHEX includes NCOs and uses a deadline date.
• **New `Whale` Definition:** The `Whale` filter will be redefined by database size (e.g., \>10k invites) to enable strategic analysis of revenue concentration, replacing the legacy definition.
• **Actionable Mailer Segmentation:** The dashboard will add filters for mailer type (physical vs. email) and sender (NSLS vs. campus) to identify performance drivers.

*Action items*
• Add mailer-type filters (physical, email-us, email-school); then add postage filters if RR differs
• Add dynamic database-size filter; then align whale threshold w/ Ashley
• Verify churn model math; then add hover tooltips w/ formulas
• Sync w/ Ashley re: HubSpot new-school revenue; then implement manual weekly updates

127271846.title: Anish / Jordan / Adam
127271846.body:

_With:_ Adam Stone, jtannenbaum@nsls.org, Anish Patel
https://fathom.video/share/b6LDytJu_vTqsQFrFVixcpy35eL26tu6

*Takeaways*
• **Goal:** Analyze response rates by mailer type (email vs. physical) to diagnose a year-over-year decline.
• **Blocker:** The dashboard's chapter-level grain prevents accurate analysis of checklist-level attributes like mailer type.
• **Solution:** Jordan will implement a temporary fix (chapter-level mailer filters) while researching a permanent, checklist-level data model.

*Action items*
• Investigate alumni revenue sources/users; map Feather fields to categories; share logic w/ Anish; publish Hex update
• Audit chapter vs checklist revenue attribution; confirm all revenue captured
• Fix Hex dashboard light mode background
• Email Kevin re: loop in Juan; pair Jordan w/ Juan for attribution/data quality

126837102.title: SLT Standing Meeting
126837102.body:
:clipboard: *SLT Standing Meeting* — 2026-03-03 18:00 · 90m
_With:_ Gary Tuerack, Ashleigh Smith, Michael O'Brien, Kimberly Campbell, Kevin Prentiss, Adam Stone
https://fathom.video/share/xsBYv_JZnoY7W56FQQmQ195GufoFqnMX

*Takeaways*
• **Personal email outreach is a high-ROI lever.** A single email to American Public University (APUS) students' personal addresses drove 348 joins in one day—\~300 more than expected. A "skunk works" team will now develop a scalable strategy.
• **MEX is evolving into a strategic asset.** The team is shifting from reactive support to proactive insights, using AI (Claude Code) to analyze customer data and inform product/marketing strategy.
• **Hiring is a critical blocker.** The current process is failing to attract engineering talent due to low salary ranges ($100k–$120k vs. a needed $125k–$165k) and slow screening. The SARS program is also delaying offers.

*Action items*
• Send MEX deck to SLT
• Review Capella inbox w/ Ashleigh; decide MEX vs Capella ownership
• Audit IVR/phone tree as a student; tighten routing to MEX
• Run contact-source/topic report (counts+%); share w/ Kevin
• Schedule sync w/ Kimberly + Ashleigh re: brand rubric L3 + dashboard
• Meet Jordan re: pre-sale pipeline inclusion in HACS

_With:_ Adam Stone, jtannenbaum@nsls.org, Anish Patel
https://fathom.video/share/7W8GSwJNG4rQnnhyoJe8L7JTaqAnH1Aa

*Takeaways*
• **Prioritize Actionable Reports:** Focus on reports that enable immediate business decisions (e.g., Growth team's invite throughput) over historical analysis (e.g., CS team's past season performance).
• **Rebuild Trust with Gary:** Address Gary's distrust by explaining the new methodology and reconciling discrepancies with the legacy "Feather" report. A weekly sync will be established.
• **Fix Foundational Data:** Resolve critical data issues like the $300-400k in "Over the Transom" (OTT) revenue and the "Feather vs. HEX" dashboard mismatch.

*Action items*

• Implement Chapter Health filters (NCO/Alumni/New/CS; PD/SPD; exclude prior-season no-sends); remove 14 speculative new chapters

• Audit Feather vs Hex response-rate mismatch w/ Anish; add transaction-date filter

126472957.title: SLT Huddle
126472957.body:
:clipboard: *SLT Huddle* — 2026-03-02 18:00 · 30m
_With:_ Gary Tuerack, Ashleigh Smith, Michael O'Brien, Kevin Prentiss, Adam Stone, Anish Patel
https://fathom.video/share/7sKBzs-YEP9rVgcEcH_B7vC_GrxiHFnw

*Takeaways*
• **Financials:** Revenue is strong, exceeding budget by \>$560k. However, ARPM is down \~$7 from budget ($133 vs. \~$140), creating a \~$700k revenue gap.
• **A\&E Upsell:** A test revealed a confusing A\&E upsell flow that appears to be a required re-purchase, likely driving accidental buys. Fixing this will lower ARPM but improve brand trust.
• **New Chapter:** Duke University is now a Registered Student Organization (RSO), a major win after years of effort. Its large grad student population (11k of 17k) offers a high-response-rate opportunity.

*Action items*
• Reconstruct Corey Powell docs; request records from SLT
• Review Allison Trachtenberg incentive program
• Send Aramark coffee order to Anish for signature; place order
• Update T&Cs: add right to request full student body list + move data ownership up
• Solidify Duke chapter start; secure student list
• Email Michael O'Brien iHeartMedia contact re: geotargeting

125716201.title: SLT Huddle
125716201.body:

_With:_ Gary Tuerack, Ashleigh Smith, Michael O'Brien, Kevin Prentiss, Adam Stone, Anish Patel
https://fathom.video/share/ZYs4D4Q8TTy71mNWBHhEbGXx9odB9ASn

*Takeaways*
• **SARs Plan Blocked:** The SARs distribution plan is stalled pending a company valuation report (due mid-next week). This prevents finalizing a compensation package for key hire David Adams, who requires a concrete SARs offer.
• **Dashboard Projections Understated:** Current financial projections are conservative, understating revenue by excluding the pipeline of 14 new schools and failing to model their higher response rates. The dashboard will be updated to pull live deal data from HubSpot for accuracy.
• **Alumni Strategy Launching:** A new alumni strategy is launching, targeting strategic accounts and new schools. A key tactic is starting with alumni chapters at new schools to avoid cannibalization from existing collegiate chapters.

*Action items*

• Email David Adams re: SARS timeline; extend old contract
• Email Adam dashboard notes/changes
• Email Michael O'Brien A&E/college-credit join flow proposal
• Email Kevin Prentiss B2B strategy notes

125444045.title: Jordan / Anish / Adam
125444045.body:
:clipboard: *Jordan / Anish / Adam* — 2026-02-25 22:00 · 60m
_With:_ Adam Stone, jtannenbaum@nsls.org, Anish Patel
https://fathom.video/share/FFLUFBS2qzYYedhyP5rV-tJLku4iEnms

*Takeaways*
• **Projection Gap:** The model's $1.9M revenue gap is due to missing new chapters (NCOs, alumni, etc.), which have high response rates (\~8%) and represent a $600k revenue opportunity.
• **Data Mismatch:** Gary's 4.6% response rate is for a narrow segment (past-deadline, no alumni/NCOs), while the model's 4.2% average includes all chapters, causing confusion.
• **New Baseline:** A raw data export will be created to establish a single source of truth, pausing UI development until the underlying data is validated.

*Action items*
• Export baseline CSV (season-level + daily joins/revenue) w/ NCO/CS/Alumni/New/Online/JHL/Email-only; share w/ Anish
• Verify per-chapter mailer type consistency within seasons; document edge cases

125300972.body:

_With:_ Jenna Fontanez, Heather Darnell, Danielle Mariani, Adam Stone, Joe Cupka, Anish Patel
https://fathom.video/share/g1d-XAExDQyMXmUaDGDEwiPWzMed_6ax

*Takeaways*
• **Decision:** The OA team restructuring is set for **Monday, March 16th**, to provide a full work week for internal communications and support, avoiding a Friday announcement that would leave staff in limbo over the weekend.
• **Critical Path:** The new OA automation tool must be finalized before the 16th. If it's not ready, the transition will be significantly more difficult for the MEX team.
• **Leadership Plan:** Kimberly (MEX) will absorb the OA team's email duties. To balance the restructuring news, several high-performing employees (LaShondra, Jordan, Dasha, Rachel) will receive concurrent title and comp upgrades.

*Action items*
• Draft project timeline for Mar 16; send to Adam, Joe, Heather, Jenna
• Review OA SOPs; send gaps/needs to Danielle
• Schedule Kimberly briefing re: OA email transition; include Danielle
• Draft updated JDs for LaShondra, Jordan, Dasha; send to Heather
• Move Marcus to LaShondra reporting
• Email DTIC re: OA Gmail/alias forwarding to help desk; request pre-provisioning

124909822.title: SLT Standing Meeting
124909822.body:

_With:_ Gary Tuerack, Ashleigh Smith, Michael O'Brien, Kevin Prentiss, Adam Stone, Anish Patel
https://fathom.video/share/atx_iMHEHVGVs1ZYgfoeLmBJoJaEcZmh

*Takeaways*
• **Response rate is down at top schools,** but this is driven by specific checklist changes (e.g., excluding high-response grad students), not a universal decline. The focus is shifting from the blended metric to analyzing granular, chapter-level data to find actionable levers.
• **The new LOP dashboard is live,** providing a single source of truth for L2 goals. The team will share status updates and comment narratives with the company for transparency and will automate L3 updates via Slack.
• **The member lifecycle strategy is being overhauled** to increase LTV to $145. This includes condensing surveys from 8 to 3 and creating a "red flag" member sequence to proactively engage at-risk members.

*Action items*

• Infer L1 statuses from L2s; confirm w/ owners
• Break automated invite workflow into L3s; assign to David

• Coordinate w/ Daniel to enable Slack updates for L3s

*Takeaways*
• **Tyler Laggerty Conversion:** A $40k GoFractional invoice for Tyler Laggerty's conversion is disputed. The agency's stated fee is 15%, making the amount excessive.
• **Equipment Policies Approved:** New policies are approved: a lifetime $200 reimbursement for walking pads (after 1 year employment) and allowing employees with 3+ years' tenure to keep their wiped laptops upon termination.
